ogTitle: Webb Beach vs Weeroona Island: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Webb Beach and Weeroona Island.
heading: Webb Beach vs Weeroona Island — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Webb Beach, South Australia and Weeroona Island, South Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Webb Beach has a lower overall climate risk score (34/100) compared to Weeroona Island (36/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Webb Beach or Weeroona Island safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Webb Beach has a flood risk score of 30/100, while Weeroona Island scores 36/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Webb Beach scores 38/100 for bushfire risk. Weeroona Island scores 45/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Webb Beach or Weeroona Island?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Webb Beach has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.9% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
